what_it_does: Renders an interactive polar SVG wheel visualization of a Projection: sectors, concentric rings, projected points, directed typed edges, strain lines between structural and semantic placement, a dyadic square/circle grid, theme labels, a touch hit-layer, a rung ladder, and a sector connection readout.
observations: Uses a decorative point layer with pointer-events:none and a separate transparent hit layer with r=15 for tapping.
Stamps ui:// addresses on SVG elements via a stamp helper.
pointAddress mints ui://canvas/seq-<n> addresses for points that lack a real ui:// address.
themeName prefers the leaf of scale_exemplar, otherwise strips the trailing count from summary.
Edges are rendered as directed chords with source dots and arrowheads; bidirectional edges get arrowheads at both ends.
Strain lines are signed: warm when meaning radius exceeds structural radius, cool when it is nearer the center.
The grid layer draws an inscribed circle inside a square, dyadic grid lines, axes, and diamond coincidence markers where rings cross axes.
Sector tap dispatches a custom window event projection:aim rather than opening a content face.
A connection readout shows in/out counts and labels pure sources/sinks as findings.
Theme labels are shown only at the coarse rung when there are 12 or fewer themes.
standards: The hit-layer/decorative-layer split for tiny touch targets.
Minting canvas addresses for unaddressed points while preserving real ui:// addresses.
Signed warm/cool strain coloring to show direction of structure-meaning divergence.
The coincidence-spine motif tying the square grid and circular rings to addressable axial points.
Pure source/sink finding readout for directional edge analysis.
conventions: React functional component with named exports and TypeScript prop typing.
Uses CSS custom properties for color tokens.
Uses framer-motion AnimatePresence and motion elements for enter/move/exit transitions driven by a MotionFeel token.
Helper functions are exported and documented with inline comments.
SVG layering order conveys visual priority: grid, rings, wedges, edges, strain, points, labels, hit layer, origin.
concerns: Direct window.dispatchEvent for projection:aim couples the component to a global event contract without local typing.
A new Map(proj.sectors...) is built on every render.
Inline IIFE blocks inside JSX for grid and readout reduce readability.
No visible keyboard or screen-reader interaction beyond role='img' and aria-label; the hit layer is mouse/touch only.
proj.count is referenced but not destructured from props, relying on the projection object shape.
notes: This is the main wheel renderer: it turns a Projection into a calm, geometric SVG instrument and handles sector aiming, point picking, and scale-rung navigation.
questions: What is the exact contract/schema for the custom projection:aim event consumers expect?
How are MotionFeel values defined and what transition durations do they map to?
What does proj.center represent when it is not the string 'now'?
Are scale_size and scale_exemplar always present together for theme centroids?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to render a polar projection instrument, and the file does exactly that, including the grid, edges, strain, and interaction layers.
apparent_themes: Meaning encoded as geometry and color.
Layered interaction separating display from hit targets.
intention_signals: It tries to make abstract projection data feel like a navigable scientific instrument where every visible mark has an address, every divergence has a sign, and every sector has a readable name and directional role.
novelty: The coincidence-spine grid motif, signed strain lines with warm/cool directionality, and the pure source/sink connection readout are unusual for a typical radial chart component.
contribution: A concrete, addressable SVG rendering substrate for projection data that combines polar geometry, directed graph overlays, signed strain visualization, coarse theme labeling, and a touch-friendly selection layer.
summary_for_embedding: Wheel.tsx is a React SVG component that renders an interactive polar projection wheel with sectors, rings, points, directed edges, strain spokes, a dyadic grid, and scale-rung controls.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a legible, addressable, and tappable wheel view of code/design projections that surfaces structural-semantic divergence and thematic clusters.

symbols.does: Returns a canonical ui:// address for a point, minting a canvas sequence address when no real ui:// address exists.
symbols.description: This helper normalizes point identity for the wheel: if the point already carries a ui:// address it is returned unchanged, otherwise a synthetic ui://canvas/seq-<seq> address is generated so every rendered mark remains addressable.

symbols.does: Produces a readable name for a coarse theme centroid from its exemplar path or summary text.
symbols.description: When a cluster centroid has a scale_exemplar, the function returns the file/path leaf; otherwise it strips the trailing membership count from the summary so the headline rung tells the user what the system chose.

symbols.does: Renders the full interactive wheel SVG and its surrounding controls/readout.
symbols.description: The Wheel component measures its container, computes polar geometry, draws the grid/rings/wedges/edges/strain/points/labels, attaches stamped addresses and click handlers, dispatches projection:aim events for sector taps, renders the RungLadder, and shows a directional connection readout for the selected sector.
